summary: A deep caries can lead to chronic apical periodontitis, resulting in a radiopaque mass seen on the radiograph due to healed inflammatory tissue surrounding the affected root tip. Given the patient’s asymptomatic state without any sign of current pathology, it aligns better with old residual effects post-infection compared to conditions requiring intervention such as abscesses or cyst formation.
